content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äã of the integers 111 through NNN. You may perform up to KKK operations of the following type:


Choose three pairwise distinct valid indices i1,i2,i3i_1, i_2, i_3i1‚Äã,i2‚Äã,i3‚Äã. Note that these indices do not have to be chosen in increasing order.
Let's denote the values of pi1p_{i_1}pi1‚Äã‚Äã, pi2p_{i_2}pi2‚Äã‚Äã and pi3p_{i_3}pi3‚Äã‚Äã before this operation by v1v_1v1‚Äã, v2v_2v2‚Äã and v3v_3v3‚Äã respectively.
Change pi2p_{i_2}pi2‚Äã‚Äã to v1v_1v1‚Äã, pi3p_{i_3}pi3‚Äã‚Äã to v2v_2v2‚Äã and pi1p_{i_1}pi1‚Äã‚Äã to v3v_3v3‚Äã. In other words, perform a cyclic right shift on the elements pi1,pi2,pi3p_{i_1}, p_{i_2}, p_{i_3}pi1‚Äã‚Äã,pi2‚Äã‚Äã,pi3‚Äã‚Äã in this order.
For example, the permutation (1,4,3,2,5)(1,4,3,2,5)(1,4,3,2,5) can be changed to the permutation (1,5,3,4,2)(1,5,3,4,2)(1,5,3,4,2) in one operation by choosing i1=2i_1 = 2i1‚Äã=2, i2=4i_2 = 4i2‚Äã=4 and i3=5i_3 = 5i3‚Äã=5.

Your task is to sort the permutation in increasing order. Note that you do not have to minimise the number of performed operations.
constraints: 1‚â§pi‚â§N1 \le p_i \le N1‚â§pi‚Äã‚â§N for each valid iii
p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äã are pairwise distinct
the sum of all NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105
pi=N‚àíi+1p_i = N-i+1pi‚Äã=N‚àíi+1 for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers p1,p2,‚Ä¶,pNp_1, p_2, \ldots, p_Np1‚Äã,p2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,pN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If it is impossible to sort the given permutation using at most KKK operations, print a single line containing the integer ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
Otherwise, first, print a line containing a single integer MMM (0‚â§M‚â§K0 \le M \le K0‚â§M‚â§K) ‚Äï the number of operations you want to perform.
Then, print MMM lines describing these operations. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§M1 \le i \le M1‚â§i‚â§M), the iii-th of these lines should contain three space-separated integers aia_iai‚Äã, bib_ibi‚Äã and cic_ici‚Äã (pairwise distinct; 1‚â§ai,bi,ci‚â§N1 \le a_i, b_i, c_i \le N1‚â§ai‚Äã,bi‚Äã,ci‚Äã‚â§N) ‚Äï the indices on which the iii-th operation is performed.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We can sort the permutation by performing one cyclic right shift operation on the indices 111, 333 and 444.
